gene_full_name: Potassium Voltage-Gated Channel Subfamily H Member 2
description: KCNH2 rs1805123 (K897T) variant associated with altered cardiac repolarization and QT interval duration. This common polymorphism affects the hERG potassium channel, predisposing carriers to prolonged QT and increased risk of drug-induced torsades de pointes.

source.title: Common genetic variation in the cardiac ion channel hERG (KCNH2) and QTc prolongation
source.authors: Bezzina CR, Verkerk AO, Buxton AE, et al.

TT.phenotype: High genetic risk for QT prolongation and drug-induced torsades de pointes
TT.effect: High genetic risk for QT prolongation and drug-induced torsades de pointes
TT.recommendation: Avoid or use with extreme caution: sotalol, amiodarone, dofetilide, erythromycin, haloperidol, methadone. Baseline and periodic ECG monitoring recommended. Electrolyte management (especially potassium, magnesium) important.

TC.phenotype: Moderate increased risk of QT prolongation with QT-prolonging drugs
TC.effect: Moderate increased risk of QT prolongation with QT-prolonging drugs
TC.recommendation: Use QT-prolonging drugs cautiously. Consider alternative agents when possible. ECG baseline and monitoring recommended, especially with multiple QT-prolonging drugs.

CT.phenotype: Moderate increased risk of QT prolongation with QT-prolonging drugs
CT.effect: Moderate increased risk of QT prolongation with QT-prolonging drugs
CT.recommendation: Use QT-prolonging drugs cautiously. Consider alternative agents when possible. ECG baseline and monitoring recommended, especially with multiple QT-prolonging drugs.

CC.phenotype: Normal QT interval risk; no specific KCNH2-related concerns
CC.effect: Normal QT interval risk; no specific KCNH2-related concerns
CC.recommendation: Standard dosing and monitoring for QT-prolonging drugs as per clinical practice
